messages.content:
During cellular respiration which of the following best describes what happens to the protons?

They are pumped into the cell before flowing back out through the ATP synthase.


They are pumped out of the cell before flowing back in through the ATP synthase.

 

They are pumped out of the ATP synthase before flowing back in through the electron transport chain.


They are transferred along a chain of enzymes and used to pump electrons out of the cell.
They are pumped out of the cell before flowing back in through the ATP synthase.

All of the following are TRUE of anaerobic respiration, EXCEPT:
Sulfate is a possible terminal electron acceptor.
Nitrate is a possible terminal electron acceptor.
 
Oxygen is a possible terminal electron acceptor.
The process generates a proton gradient.
The process generates a proton gradient.

Which of the following statements about anaerobic respiration is TRUE?
Anaerobic respiration is rare because most bacteria live in oxygen conditions.
Anaerobic respiration uses an electron transport chain to generate a proton motive force.
 
In electron transport chains used for anaerobic respiration, every pathway uses the same enzymes.
Every pathway in anaerobic respiration must start with glycolysis.
Anaerobic respiration uses an electron transport chain to generate a proton motive force.

A proton motive force is
The process of generating energy by using sulfate or nitrate as a terminal electron acceptor
An energized state across a membrane resulting from a proton gradient
The process of combining ADP and a phosphate to produce ATP
 
An energized state across a membrane resulting from an electron gradient
The process of generating energy by using oxygen as a terminal electron acceptor
An energized state across a membrane resulting from a proton gradient.

What is the end goal of both aerobic and anaerobic respiration?
Generation of water by reducing oxygen.
Generation of pyruvate by glycolysis.
 
Generation of ATP via oxidative phosphorylation.
Generation of an electron motive force.
Generation of ATP via oxidative phosphorylation.

What is the end goal of both aerobic and anaerobic respiration?
Generation of water by reducing oxygen.
Generation of pyruvate by glycolysis.
 
Generation of ATP via oxidative phosphorylation.
Generation of an electron motive force.
Generation of ATP via oxidative phosphorylation.

During cellular respiration which of the following best describes what happens to the protons?

They are pumped into the cell before flowing back out through the ATP synthase.


They are pumped out of the cell before flowing back in through the ATP synthase.

 

They are pumped out of the ATP synthase before flowing back in through the electron transport chain.


They are transferred along a chain of enzymes and used to pump electrons out of the cell.
They are pumped out of the cell before flowing back in through the ATP synthase.

All of the following are TRUE of anaerobic respiration, EXCEPT:
Sulfate is a possible terminal electron acceptor.
Nitrate is a possible terminal electron acceptor.
 
Oxygen is a possible terminal electron acceptor.
The process generates a proton gradient.
Oxygen is a possible terminal electron acceptor.

Which of the following statements about anaerobic respiration is TRUE?
Anaerobic respiration is rare because most bacteria live in oxygen conditions.
Anaerobic respiration uses an electron transport chain to generate a proton motive force.
 
In electron transport chains used for anaerobic respiration, every pathway uses the same enzymes.
Every pathway in anaerobic respiration must start with glycolysis.
Anaerobic respiration uses an electron transport chain to generate a proton motive force.

A proton motive force is
The process of generating energy by using sulfate or nitrate as a terminal electron acceptor
An energized state across a membrane resulting from a proton gradient
The process of combining ADP and a phosphate to produce ATP
 
An energized state across a membrane resulting from an electron gradient
The process of generating energy by using oxygen as a terminal electron acceptor
An energized state across a membrane resulting from a proton gradient.

What is the end goal of both aerobic and anaerobic respiration?
Generation of water by reducing oxygen.
Generation of pyruvate by glycolysis.
 
Generation of ATP via oxidative phosphorylation.
Generation of an electron motive force.
The end goal of both aerobic and anaerobic respiration is the generation of ATP via oxidative phosphorylation.

Describing fermentation as an anaerobic process means that _____

it is spontaneous and doesn't require enzymes.


it is dependent on the presence of oxygen.


it is killed by oxygen.

 

oxygen is not necessary for the process.


it is a result of a proton motive force.
Describing fermentation as an anaerobic process means that oxygen is not necessary for the process.
